div1.entryFree:
Bonny, 1) blithe, cheerful: be you blithe and b. Ado II, 3, 69. b. Kate, Shr. II, 187. III, 2, 229. b. sweet Robin, Hml. IV, 5, 187. a cherry lip, a b. eye, R3 I, 1, 94.
2) stout, strong: made a prey for carrion kites and crows even of the b. beast he loved so well, H6B V, 2, 12. the b. prizer of the humorous duke, As II, 3, 8 (most M. Edd. bony).
